meta.name: Ukraine
meta.description: Ukraine has a long academic tradition with over 400 higher education institutions. Universities offer affordable tuition and strong programs in engineering, medicine, and sciences.

tips: Many Ukrainian universities offer programs in English, especially in medicine and engineering.
Tuition fees are among the lowest in Europe — public universities charge $1,000–3,000/year for international students.
Kyiv-Mohyla Academy and KPI are considered the most prestigious universities in Ukraine.
EU citizens may study at Ukrainian universities; check current entry requirements.
Ukraine uses the 100-point grading scale; 60+ is a passing grade.

universities.name: Taras Shevchenko National University of Kyiv
universities.desc: Ukraine's most prestigious classical university, founded in 1834. Strong in humanities, natural sciences, and law. The main symbol of Ukrainian higher education.

universities.name: Igor Sikorsky Kyiv Polytechnic Institute
universities.desc: Ukraine's top technical university. Produces the majority of Ukraine's engineers and IT specialists. Named after aviation pioneer Igor Sikorsky.

universities.name: National University of Kyiv-Mohyla Academy
universities.desc: One of the oldest universities in Eastern Europe, refounded in 1991. Known for academic freedom, liberal arts education, and producing Ukraine's intellectual elite.

universities.name: V.N. Karazin Kharkiv National University
universities.desc: One of Ukraine's oldest and most respected universities. Known for producing Nobel laureates and strong research in physics and chemistry.

universities.name: Ivan Franko National University of Lviv
universities.desc: The oldest university in western Ukraine, founded in 1661. A center of Ukrainian culture and national identity with strong programs in humanities and law.

universities.name: Lviv Polytechnic National University
universities.desc: Western Ukraine's leading technical university. One of Ukraine's largest universities with strong IT and engineering programs, popular among international students.

universities.name: Odesa I.I. Mechnikov National University
universities.desc: Odesa's main classical university, named after Nobel laureate Ilya Mechnikov. Strong in natural sciences and known for its beautiful historic campus.

universities.name: Ukrainian Catholic University
universities.desc: Ukraine's most selective private university. Western-style liberal arts education with English-taught programs. Strong alumni network and international partnerships.

universities.name: National Aviation University
universities.desc: Ukraine's main aviation university. Offers aerospace engineering, air traffic control, and related fields. Popular with international students for English-medium programs.

universities.name: Kyiv National Economic University
universities.desc: Ukraine's top economics university. Produces the majority of Ukraine's economists and financial specialists. Strong ties with government and business sectors.

universities.name: National Pedagogical Dragomanov University
universities.desc: Ukraine's largest pedagogical university, producing teachers and educators. Affordable and accessible with a wide range of programs.

universities.name: Bogomolets National Medical University
universities.desc: Ukraine's oldest and most prestigious medical university. Attracts many international students with English-medium medical programs at affordable prices.

universities.name: Kharkiv National Polytechnic Institute
universities.desc: Kharkiv's leading technical university. Strong in energy engineering, mechanical engineering, and computer science. Long tradition of technical education in eastern Ukraine.

universities.name: Kharkiv National University of Radio Electronics
universities.desc: Specializes in IT, electronics, and communications. Known for strong industry connections and producing top software engineers and electronics specialists.

universities.name: Kharkiv National Agrarian University
universities.desc: One of Ukraine's oldest agricultural universities. Strong in agronomy, veterinary medicine, and agricultural economics.

universities.name: Oles Honchar Dnipro National University
universities.desc: Dnipro's main classical university. Strong in physics and aerospace-related sciences, reflecting the city's historical role in Ukraine's space and defense industries.

universities.name: Dnipro University of Technology
universities.desc: Ukraine's oldest mining and geological engineering university. Specializes in mining, geotechnics, and industrial engineering.

universities.name: Odesa National Maritime University
universities.desc: Ukraine's main maritime university. Trains ship captains, marine engineers, and maritime lawyers. Located in the Black Sea port city of Odesa.

universities.name: Odesa State Environmental University
universities.desc: Specializes in environmental sciences, meteorology, and hydrology. Unique in Ukraine for its focus on Black Sea and Azov Sea ecological studies.

universities.name: Kyiv National University of Construction and Architecture
universities.desc: Ukraine's leading university for architecture and civil engineering. Popular with students from the Middle East and Africa for English-medium programs.

universities.name: O.M. Beketov National University of Urban Economy
universities.desc: Specializes in urban economy, municipal management, and architecture. Strong practical focus with internships in Kharkiv's city administration.

universities.name: Sumy State University
universities.desc: A comprehensive university known for active international recruitment. Offers medicine and engineering programs in English at very affordable prices.

universities.name: Vasyl Stefanyk Precarpathian National University
universities.desc: Western Ukraine's classical university serving the Carpathian region. Strong in humanities and teacher education.

universities.name: Cherkasy State Technological University
universities.desc: A growing technological university in central Ukraine. Known for affordable IT and engineering programs.

universities.name: Vinnytsia National Technical University
universities.desc: A strong technical university in Podolia. Produces engineers for Ukraine's energy and electronics sectors.

universities.name: Zaporizhzhia National University
universities.desc: The main classical university of southern Ukraine's industrial city. Comprehensive programs in humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences.

universities.name: Zaporizhzhia National Technical University
universities.desc: Technical university in Ukraine's industrial heartland. Strong in metallurgy, mechanical engineering, and industrial automation.

universities.name: Yuriy Fedkovych Chernivtsi National University
universities.desc: Chernivtsi's historic university with a stunning UNESCO World Heritage campus (former Metropolitans' Residence). Strong in humanities and Central European studies.

universities.name: National University of Water and Environmental Engineering
universities.desc: Unique specialization in water resources and hydraulic engineering. Located in Rivne, serving the Volyn and Polesia regions.

universities.name: Ternopil Ivan Puluj National Technical University
universities.desc: A technical university in western Ukraine named after physicist Ivan Puluj. Known for engineering and food technology programs.

universities.name: Kyiv National Linguistic University
universities.desc: Ukraine's main university for foreign language education and translation. Graduates work as diplomats, translators, and language teachers worldwide.

universities.name: Kyiv University of Law of the National Academy of Sciences
universities.desc: A specialized law university affiliated with the National Academy of Sciences. Produces top legal scholars and practitioners.

universities.name: Kyiv National University of Technologies and Design
universities.desc: Ukraine's main university for design, fashion, and light industry. Strong in fashion design and textile engineering.

universities.name: Kharkiv National Medical University
universities.desc: One of Ukraine's oldest and most respected medical universities. Very popular with international students from India and Africa for English-medium medicine.

universities.name: Odesa National Medical University
universities.desc: A historic medical university in the Black Sea port city. One of the most internationally diverse medical schools in Ukraine.

universities.name: Danylo Halytsky Lviv National Medical University
universities.desc: One of Eastern Europe's oldest medical universities, tracing origins to 1784. Located in historic Lviv; strong in clinical medicine and pharmacy.

universities.name: University of State Fiscal Service of Ukraine
universities.desc: Specializes in taxation, customs, and fiscal policy. Trains specialists for Ukraine's tax and customs services.

universities.name: Interregional Academy of Personnel Management
universities.desc: Ukraine's largest private university by enrollment. Wide range of evening and distance learning programs targeting working adults.

universities.name: H.S. Skovoroda Kharkiv National Pedagogical University
universities.desc: One of Ukraine's oldest pedagogical universities. Trains teachers across all disciplines for schools in eastern Ukraine.

universities.name: Kremenchuk Mykhailo Ostrohradskyi National University
universities.desc: A technical university in the Poltava region serving the automotive and industrial sectors of central Ukraine.

universities.name: Central Ukrainian National Technical University
universities.desc: The main technical university of central Ukraine. Specializes in agricultural machinery engineering and industrial automation.

universities.name: Dnipro State Medical University
universities.desc: One of the oldest medical universities in eastern Ukraine. Offers English-medium medicine at competitive prices for international students.

universities.name: Poltava V.G. Korolenko National Pedagogical University
universities.desc: Poltava's main pedagogical university, serving the educational needs of the Poltava region.

universities.name: Kherson State University
universities.desc: The main university of southern Ukraine's Kherson region. Strong in biology and ecological sciences related to the Dnipro delta.

universities.name: Uzhhorod National University
universities.desc: Westernmost Ukrainian university, near the Slovakian and Hungarian borders. Has Hungarian-language programs and strong cross-border European connections.

universities.name: Odesa State Academy of Civil Engineering and Architecture
universities.desc: Odesa's specialist university for architecture and construction. Known for its architectural heritage programs and English-language courses.

universities.name: National University of Life and Environmental Sciences of Ukraine
universities.desc: Ukraine's main agricultural university on a beautiful forested campus in Kyiv. Strong in agronomy, environmental science, and veterinary medicine.

universities.name: University KROK
universities.desc: A modern private university focused on business and law. Known for practical education and strong employer connections in Kyiv's business community.

universities.name: Ivano-Frankivsk National Technical University of Oil and Gas
universities.desc: Ukraine's only university specializing in oil and gas engineering. Located near the Carpathian oil fields; graduates work in energy companies across Eastern Europe.

universities.name: Lutsk National Technical University
universities.desc: A technical university in northwest Ukraine's Volyn region. Focus on automotive engineering and environmental technologies.

universities.name: Simon Kuznets Kharkiv National University of Economics
universities.desc: One of Ukraine's leading economics universities outside Kyiv. Named after Nobel laureate Simon Kuznets who was born in Kharkiv.

universities.name: Sumy State University
universities.desc: Sumy's comprehensive public university — strong in electrical engineering, medicine and informatics, known for an unusually international teaching environment.

universities.name: Kherson State University
universities.desc: The historic university of Ukraine's southern port — strong in biology, ecology and teacher education before the full-scale invasion displaced many students northward.

universities.name: H.S. Skovoroda Kharkiv National Pedagogical University
universities.desc: Ukraine's oldest teacher education university named after philosopher Hryhoriy Skovoroda — training teachers in humanities, arts and social sciences since 1811.

universities.name: Chernivtsi National University
universities.desc: UNESCO World Heritage campus in former Habsburg Bukovina — Austria's most extravagant university architecture, with strong Romanian studies and unique Central European history.

universities.name: Kharkiv National University of Radio Electronics
universities.desc: Ukraine's leading university for electronics, telecommunications and computer engineering — producing engineers who fill Kharkiv's internationally recognised IT cluster.

universities.name: Kharkiv National Automobile and Highway University
universities.desc: Ukraine's specialist transport engineering university — training the road and logistics engineers who are critical for Ukraine's wartime supply chain infrastructure.

universities.name: Dnipro University of Technology
universities.desc: Ukraine's oldest mining engineering university — now pivoting to IT, renewable energy and materials engineering alongside its traditional geoscience and mining strengths.

universities.name: Poltava National Technical Yuri Kondratyuk University
universities.desc: Named after the forgotten genius Yuri Kondratyuk — the Ukrainian who calculated the Apollo moon trajectory. Strong in civil engineering, oil gas extraction and architecture.

universities.name: Kharkiv National University of Arts
universities.desc: Eastern Ukraine's leading fine and performing arts conservatory — the most selective arts institution outside Kyiv, with world-class opera and ballet training.

universities.name: Lviv Polytechnic National University – 2
universities.desc: Ukraine's largest technical university with 18 institutes and 100+ degree programmes — particularly strong in ICT, embedded systems and architectural design.

universities.name: Ukrainian Free University
universities.desc: Founded in exile in Vienna in 1921, now operating from Munich — an important institution for the Ukrainian diaspora offering Ukrainian-language degrees in the humanities and law.

universities.name: Borys Grinchenko Kyiv Metropolitan University
universities.desc: Kyiv's municipal university — named after the Ukrainian linguist and educator, strong in teacher education, Ukrainian studies and digital humanities.

universities.name: National Pedagogical Dragomanov University
universities.desc: Ukraine's leading teacher education university — training the teachers of tomorrow for Ukrainian schools, with particular strength in Ukrainian language pedagogy.

universities.name: State University of Trade and Economics
universities.desc: Ukraine's specialist commerce and trade university — training accountants, economists and retail managers who form the backbone of Ukraine's private sector.

universities.name: National Aviation University Kyiv
universities.desc: Ukraine's national aviation and aerospace university — training the pilots, aerospace engineers and air traffic controllers who support Ukrainian aviation and space industry.

universities.name: National Forestry University of Ukraine
universities.desc: Ukraine's historic forestry and wood technology university — managing Carpathian forests, training environmental engineers and architects in sustainable wood construction.

universities.name: Ukrainian Catholic University – extended
universities.desc: Ukraine's most innovative private university — consistently ranked first among Ukrainian private universities, producing civil society leaders, IT entrepreneurs and theologians.

universities.name: Berdiansk State Pedagogical University
universities.desc: Now evacuated from occupied Berdyansk — operating in Zaporizhzhia, continuing teacher education for the children of internally displaced people from the Azov coast.

universities.name: Kharkiv National Agrarian University
universities.desc: Ukraine's oldest agrarian university — training agricultural engineers and soil scientists critical for feeding Ukraine and the world from the breadbasket of Europe.

universities.name: Poltava State Agrarian University
universities.desc: The farming heartland's university — specialising in crop science, land management and veterinary medicine in Ukraine's most fertile black earth region.

universities.name: Odessa Polytechnic National University
universities.desc: The Black Sea city's technical university — strong in electrical engineering, shipbuilding and automation, with Ukraine's largest student IT campus outside Kyiv.

universities.name: Odessa I.I. Mechnikov National University
universities.desc: Odessa's classical university named after Nobel biologist Élie Metchnikoff — strong in biology, marine sciences and international relations in the historically cosmopolitan port city.

universities.name: Luhansk National University
universities.desc: Evacuated from occupied Luhansk and now operating in Poltava — maintaining academic continuity for students from Ukraine's eastern territories under Russian occupation.

universities.name: Zhytomyr Polytechnic State University
universities.desc: The Polissia region's technical university — strong in environmental engineering, CS and construction in the historically neglected Polissia wetlands region of northern Ukraine.

universities.name: Mykolayiv National University
universities.desc: The Shipbuilding Capital's classical university — continuing to function during wartime despite Mykolayiv's proximity to the front line, with particular strength in ecology and education.

universities.name: Dnipro State Medical University
universities.desc: Eastern Ukraine's main medical university — training doctors with battlefield-relevant surgical skills, accepting international students in English-taught medicine programmes.

universities.name: Ivano-Frankivsk National Medical University
universities.desc: Western Ukraine's medical university — significant numbers of international students from India, Middle East and Africa study medicine here in English, with a clinical hospital on campus.

universities.name: Bukovinian State Medical University
universities.desc: Chernivtsi's medical university — training Ukrainian-language and international English-track medical students in a beautiful UNESCO-listed Habsburg campus.

universities.name: Ukrainian Medical Stomatological Academy
universities.desc: One of Ukraine's most popular universities for international medical students — English-taught medicine and dentistry, with thousands of students from India and Asia.

universities.name: Shupyk National Healthcare University of Ukraine
universities.desc: Ukraine's national postgraduate medical academy — retraining Ukraine's practicing doctors in modern wartime medicine, trauma surgery and oncology.

universities.name: National Technical University of Ukraine – Igor Sikorsky KPI
universities.desc: The main campus of Ukraine's technical flagship — 19 faculties training the engineers who power Ukraine's military-industrial complex, energy sector and IT industry.

universities.name: Ukrainian State University of Railway Transport
universities.desc: Ukraine's railway engineering university — training the transport engineers who keep Ukraine's 23,000 km rail network — the wartime lifeline — operational.

universities.name: Uman State Pedagogical University
universities.desc: In the city of Sofiyivka Park — the jewel of Ukrainian landscape architecture. Strong in pedagogy, arts and natural science education.

universities.name: Vasyl Stus Donetsk National University
universities.desc: Evacuated from occupied Donetsk to Vinnytsia — named after the dissident poet Vasyl Stus who died in a Soviet gulag; a university that embodies Ukrainian resistance.

universities.name: Ternopil Volodymyr Hnatiuk National Pedagogical University
universities.desc: Western Ukraine's teacher education hub — training teachers for the whole Ternopil region in humanities, natural sciences and physical education.

universities.name: Chernihiv Polytechnic National University
universities.desc: Ukraine's northernmost polytechnic, near the Russian and Belarusian borders — continuing to operate despite heavy wartime damage to the city, with strong IT and engineering faculties.

universities.name: Kyiv-Mohyla Business School
universities.desc: Ukraine's leading executive MBA school — attached to NaUKMA, training Ukraine's most senior business managers and public sector leaders in a time of national transformation.

Kyiv.desc: Ukraine's capital and largest city, home to most of the country's top universities.

Lviv.desc: Western Ukraine's cultural capital with a UNESCO-listed old town and strong European connections.

Kharkiv.desc: Ukraine's second-largest city and a major academic center with numerous universities.
highlights: Freedom Square — one of Europe's largest

Odesa.desc: Ukraine's port city on the Black Sea, known for its cosmopolitan culture and beaches.

Dnipro.desc: Ukraine's third-largest city, an industrial and aerospace center on the Dnipro River.

Zaporizhzhia.desc: Zaporizhzhia is Ukraine's industrial powerhouse on the Dnieper River — birthplace of the legendary Cossack Sich island (Khortytsia). The city has strong technical and engineering universities, very affordable student living, and a proud Cossack heritage that gives it a unique cultural identity.
highlights: Khortytsia Island — Cossack historical museum complex in the Dnieper
Dnieper Hydroelectric Station — one of Europe's largest dams
Regional Museum of Zaporizhzhia — local history and Cossack artefacts
Central Park along the Dnieper riverside
Zaporizhzhia.studentLife: National University Zaporizhzhia and Zaporizhzhia State Medical University are the main institutions. Strong engineering and medical programmes. Very affordable rents.
Zaporizhzhia.transport: City trams and trolleybuses. Train connections to Kyiv (5h), Dnipro (2h), Kharkiv (5h).
Zaporizhzhia.nightlife: City centre nightlife along Sobornyi Avenue. More local character than the western cities.
pros: Large industrial city with diverse job market

Vinnytsia.desc: Vinnytsia is one of Ukraine's most pleasant mid-sized cities — clean, modern, and safe, with one of the world's most spectacular musical fountains on the Southern Buh River. Pirogov National Medical University makes it a destination for international medical students, particularly from India, Morocco, and the Middle East.
highlights: Roshen Fountain — world's largest floating fountain and light show
Pirogov Clinic and Farmstead — historic estate of the famous surgeon
Central Park of Culture — riverside green space
Vinnytsia.studentLife: Pirogov National Medical University attracts international students from 50+ countries, making Vinnytsia surprisingly cosmopolitan for its size. Strong English-speaking international community.
Vinnytsia.transport: City trams, marshrutkas (shared minibuses), and trolleybuses. Train to Kyiv (3h), Lviv (5h).
Vinnytsia.nightlife: Pedestrianised city centre with bars and cafés. Younger, safer scene than major cities.
pros: World's largest floating fountain — genuinely spectacular
Unusually safe and clean for a Ukrainian city
